ud7.6:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud7.6:1.3:
And on that occasion Ven. Aññāta Koṇḍañña was sitting not far from the Blessed One, his legs crossed,
his body held erect, reflecting on [his] release through the total ending of craving.
ud7.6:2.1:
The Blessed One saw Ven. Aññāta Koṇḍañña sitting not far away, his legs crossed,
his body held erect, reflecting on [his] release through the total ending of craving.
ud7.6:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud7.6:4.1: For someone with no root, no soil, no leaves
ud7.6:4.2:
–how creepers?
Who’s fit to criticize him?–
ud7.6:4.3: the enlightened one freed from bonds.
